content: Just as the original Reflexion agent leverages dynamic self-reflection on past action trajectories to correct errors and improve autonomous task performance, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow the explicit, iterative dynamic memory structure from the original Reflexion framework to turn scattered inconsistent solution perspectives (as explored in current LLM reflection research) into cumulative, graded performance gains, rather than just contrasting inconsistent outputs. This creates a new path to improve LLM reasoning that moves beyond single-step contrastive reflection to iterative, memory-augmented self-improvement tied to concrete past attempts.
